_comment: Hypothetical habitable moon test for Alpha Centauri A b (the real-life Polyphemus candidate, Beichman 2025). Pandora is the fictional Na'vi homeworld from Avatar (James Cameron) — canonically the 5th moon of the gas giant Polyphemus in Alpha Centauri A's habitable zone. This tests whether an Avatar-Pandora-mass moon stays Hill-bound around the HZ-stable Polyphemus orbit (run with --acen-a-au 1.6 --acen-e 0.1 --acen-incl-deg 16 --integrator ias15).

bodies.name: Pandora
bodies._design_note: Avatar canon (updated 2026-06-15 from fan-wiki/guidebook research): mass 0.72 M_earth (4.30e24 kg), diameter 11,447 km (radius 5724 km, ~0.90 R_earth). Canon also states 0.8 g, which is mildly inconsistent with 0.72 M_earth at this radius (-> ~0.89 g); we honor the cited mass + diameter. Pandora is tidally locked to Polyphemus, so its ~27 h day = its orbital period; Kepler back-out around a 120 M_earth planet gives a = 225,000 km. That puts Polyphemus ~36 deg across Pandora's sky (the films' dramatic skyline) at ~0.02 R_Hill (R_Hill ~1.1e7 km at 1.6 AU around 1.1 M_sun) -> deep inside the Domingos+2006 ~0.5 R_Hill limit, very stable. Moon mass is negligible vs the 120 M_earth planet, so the 0.45->0.72 update does not change Hill stability. inclination_deg is relative to the planet's orbital plane (Pandora orbits in Polyphemus's equatorial plane; canon axial tilt 29 deg).
